title: Noronhia obovata
taxonomy: Noronhia obovata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Oleaceae, which is a family of plants commonly known as the olive family. This family is further divided into 24 genera, and Noronhia obovata belongs to the genus Noronhia. The species Noronhia obovata is native to the island of Madagascar, and it is a type of evergreen tree. The Oleaceae family is a group of plants that are primarily composed of trees and shrubs, and they are native to tropical and subtropical regions around the world. Noronhia obovata is a unique and endemic species found only in the forests of Madagascar.
